title: Ledebouria minor
taxonomy: Ledebouria minor is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, specifically within the subfamily Scilloideae. It belongs to the genus Ledebouria, which comprises several species of geophytes native to southern Africa. This classification places Ledebouria minor in the larger context of the asparagus-like plants. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its bulbous habit and strap-like leaves. By being classified within the Asparagaceae family, Ledebouria minor shares common ancestors with other flowering plants like hyacinths and lilies.

intro: Ledebouria minor, also known as the small-flowered cat's ear or small-flowered squill, is a small, bulbous perennial plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family. The health benefits of Ledebouria minor include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
